description: Production eval policy defaults. Process environment variables still override these values for emergency rollback. v32.5 (2026-05-13, 72-min round cap, post-B200): user-mandated 72-minute hard ceiling on a single eval round (replaces v32.4's 8h cap). Diagnosed root cause: pod_eval_vllm.py is named for vLLM but actually generates with HuggingFace `model.generate()` ONE PROMPT AT A TIME inside _bench_generate (sequential, no batching). With BENCH_ENABLE_THINKING=1 and uncapped reasoning, v31_reasoning_logic_grid alone took 2145s = 35.7 min/student on B200 (12/18 prompts, several hitting the 6144 cap as unterminating chains). At ~85 min/student steady state x 10 students per round (SINGLE_EVAL_MAX_PER_ROUND=10), rounds were projecting 18 h. To fit 72 min today (without the multi-day vLLM batching refactor): (a) SINGLE_EVAL_MAX_PER_ROUND 10 -> 1 so a round contains the king (paired re-eval) + 1 challenger (not 10). (b) Hard token caps on the slow benches dominating wall time: BENCH_V31_LOGIC_GRID_MAX_TOKENS 6144 -> 1536 (4x cap-bound speedup, ~9 min instead of 35 min), BENCH_V31_DYVAL_MAX_TOKENS / BENCH_V31_KG_MAX_TOKENS / BENCH_V31_CONSISTENCY_MAX_TOKENS 6144 -> 2048, BENCH_V31_MATH_COMPETITION_MAX_TOKENS / BENCH_V31_MATH_ROBUSTNESS_MAX_TOKENS / BENCH_ROBUSTNESS_MAX_TOKENS 8192 -> 3072, BENCH_AIME_MAX_TOKENS 8192 -> 4096. Other axes (math/code/reasoning/knowledge/ifeval/mbpp/long_context/truthfulness/code_plus) stay at 16K because they terminate well under any cap. (c) DISTIL_POD_EVAL_TIMEOUT_S 28800 -> 4320 (72 min hard ceiling). (d) DISTIL_STAGE_STALL_DEFAULT_S 3000 -> 600 (any single bench can't take >10 min before watchdog kills + SIGTERM). Trade-off acknowledged: this walks back the v32.1 'uncapped reasoning' promise on the highest-variance procedural axes (logic_grid/dyval/kg/consistency, plus the math axes). The model still gets 1.5-3K reasoning tokens per prompt (plenty for non-stuck chains) but unterminating loops can no longer dominate wall time. Quality impact: hardest-grid logic puzzles where the model needs >1.5K tokens of trace will degrade; easier instances unaffected because they terminate at <500 tokens. The right long-term fix is to refactor _run_simple_bench to use real vLLM batched generation (queue all prompts, let vLLM scheduler interleave them) which would give a 30-50x throughput multiplier and make uncapped reasoning trivially fit. Filed as TODO. v32.4 (2026-05-13, B200 hardware cutover + uncapped prompt counts): pod hardware swapped H100 PCIe -> RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Workstation (96 GB) -> NVIDIA B200 (183 GB VRAM, sm_100, ~2x faster on FP8/FP4 than RTX 6000). Earlier 2026-05-13 we briefly trimmed LOGIC_GRID/DYVAL/KG per-round prompt counts 18 -> 10 to bound a Blackwell round under 5 h, but the resulting +25% per-axis SE was unacceptable for the dethrone gate's stability. Now reverted to 18 prompts each (the full v31 default) and instead bumped eval_timeout 5h -> 8h via DISTIL_POD_EVAL_TIMEOUT_S=28800. With B200 raw throughput we expect per-student steady state ~25-30 min and a 10-student round at 4-5 h. Per-bench Blackwell timings (vs H100): math 2.83x, ifeval 2.19x, math_competition 3.0x, code_humaneval_plus 4.4x. B200 adds another ~1.5-2x on top. v32.3 (2026-05-12 broader cap, after second-stall recurrence): the v32.2 procedural-cap fix held — logic_grid/dyval/kg all completed cleanly at 6 K — but the next round saw two new stall pathologies. (a) consistency_paraphrase took 1255s for 14 prompts at 16 K (the bench fans out 5 paraphrases per prompt, multiplying any unterminating chain by 5x). (b) Foremost04/will_king_v2 hung for 30+ min on math_competition under the 16 K cap (single-prompt unterminating-derivation loop). Same fix shape as v32.2: BENCH_V31_CONSISTENCY_MAX_TOKENS 16 K -> 6 K, BENCH_V31_MATH_COMPETITION_MAX_TOKENS / BENCH_V31_MATH_ROBUSTNESS_MAX_TOKENS / BENCH_AIME_MAX_TOKENS / BENCH_ROBUSTNESS_MAX_TOKENS 16 K -> 8 K (math axes get 8 K of think+derivation, more than enough for a non-stuck chain but bounded). Stage stall watchdog _STAGE_STALL_DEFAULT_S 2400 -> 3000s to absorb the residual variance after these caps. Code/knowledge/ifeval/mbpp/long_context/truthfulness benches stay at 16 K — they terminated cleanly in the 18:45 UTC round at <300s each, so no need to compress them. v32.2 (2026-05-12 procedural cap, first stall recurrence): three procedural-reasoning axes (logic_grid, dyval, kg) capped at 6 K and watchdog bumped 1500 -> 2400 because v32.1's uniform 16 K let those benches enter unterminating reasoning loops on 4-7 B distilled students (logic_grid stalled for 1515s with prompts_done=0). v32.1 (2026-05-11 reasoning uncap, second pass): user asked 'why are we capping reasoning at all? why not make it uncapped so the model can be the best that it can be.' Every capability axis MAX_TOKENS bumped to 16 384 (16 K) — the chat pod's effective budget — so the king's <think>...</think> chain never truncates mid-reason on any capability axis. Eval pod's vLLM --max-model-len bumped 8 K -> 32 K (matches chat pod) so prompt + 16 K output fits even on the long-context axes. RULER stays at 8 K output because its prompts run 4-8 K, leaving safe headroom. LC bumped 1 K -> 8 K (its 60-distractor prompt is ~3 K). Held-out king benchmark all-axes bumped to 16 K. Chat app default max_tokens 4 K -> 16 K, ceiling 6 K -> 30 K (matches CHAT_VLLM_MAX_MODEL_LEN with 2 K headroom). The CHAT_PROBE / THINK_PROBE / CAPABILITY_PROBE budgets are STILL deliberately untouched because those probes specifically test for chat-collapse / thinking-collapse pathologies and need their tight 48-768 caps to detect the failure mode. The same Goodhart logic from the v31.2 noisy-axis retirement applies: any cap below the model's natural reasoning length artificially compresses the capability gap and rewards no-think collapse. The grading code already strips <think>...</think> before comparing answers, so a larger budget can only ADD signal and never inflate the score by letting trace text leak into the answer. v32 (2026-05-11 think-budget bump, the now-superseded first pass): every per-axis BENCH_*_MAX_TOKENS budget tripled-to-quadrupled (e.g. KNOWLEDGE 48 -> 1024) so the model has room to emit a <think>...</think> chain before the answer. Held-out king benchmark and chat app proxy flipped to enable_thinking=True; the held-out per-bench max_tokens were doubled (e.g. arc/mmlu_pro 1024 -> 2048, gsm8k/ifeval 2048 -> 4096) to give the trace room. This is the same Goodhart fix as v31.2's noisy-axis retirement: the validator rewards the king's REAL capability instead of a tightened no-think floor that hides reasoning gains. v31.3 (2026-05-10 top_k_overlap halve, research-review followup): TOP_K_OVERLAP_AXIS_WEIGHT 0.18 → 0.09 because the 2026-05-10 axis correlation audit (n=5 paired post-Kimi kings) shows top_k_overlap ↔ held-out gsm8k Pearson r = -0.481 — the largest negative correlation among any non-zero-weight axis. The published research that motivated 0.18 (Anshumann ACL 2025) demonstrated the signal at frontier scale, not 4 B distilled scale where teacher mimicry may memorise rather than generalise. ON_POLICY_RKL_WEIGHT bumped 0.30 → 0.39 to absorb the freed 0.09. RKL is the central distillation signal in the GKD-style 'On-Policy Distillation' lineage (Thinking Machines Nov 2025) — moving weight from a teacher-mimicry axis to an on-policy generalisation axis is the most defensible single change of this commit. Re-evaluate at n >= 12: if top_k_overlap correlation stays at or below 0 with a tight CI, retire to 0; if it recovers above +0.3, restore to 0.12. v31.2 (2026-05-10 noisy-axis retirement, Discord feedback from `duohuang`): two further composite-cleanup moves on top of the variance reduction. (a) ``code_skill_group`` retired (CODE_SKILL_GROUP_WEIGHT 0.05 → 0.0) — its bottom_half_mean aggregation pulls in ``refactor_bench`` (n=4), ``debug_bench`` (n=6), and ``correction_bench`` (n=6) which dominate variance without adding signal v31_code_humaneval_plus already covers. (b) ``tool_use_bench`` retired (BENCH_TOOL_USE_WEIGHT 0.10 → 0.0) — n=16 agentic-Python axis whose capability surface is already in v31_code_humaneval_plus + v31_math_competition. Both axes still RUN as telemetry (visible on dashboard, in axis_correlation.json) but no longer contribute to composite or the dethrone gate. Total raw composite weight 2.18 → 2.03 (−7%); after renormalisation each surviving axis gains ~7% effective weight, with v31's share rising 23% → 25%. Combined with v31.1's variance reduction, false-positive dethrone rate from pure noise drops to <6% per round (was ~8% post-v31.1, ~27% pre-v31.1). v31.1 (2026-05-10 variance reduction): three changes harden the dethrone gate against single-shot RNG-variance wins. (1) Per-axis n bumped on every v31 axis (n=8 → 14-16, n=12 → 18, gsm_symbolic 16 → 24) so per-axis SE at p=0.5 falls from 0.14-0.18 to 0.10-0.13 (~30% narrower). (2) Worst-K aggregator widened from K=3 to K=5 (WORST_3_MEAN_K=5) so a single chance-driven low axis can no longer dominate the composite (-23% SE on the bottom-K mean). (3) Dethrone margin SINGLE_EVAL_DETHRONE_MARGIN bumped 0.03 → 0.05 — combined with (1) and (2) the false-positive dethrone rate from pure noise drops from ~27% per round to <8%. POD_PER_MODEL_TIMEOUT bumped 1500 → 2400 to cover the longer eval (~5-7 min/student more for the 60-70% increase in v31 sample counts). v31 (2026-05-09 second pass): 11 fresh procedural axes promoted out of SHADOW and into the composite (V31_AXES_IN_COMPOSITE=1, default ON). They carry 0.50 of composite weight (0.06 v31_math_gsm_symbolic + 0.05 v31_math_competition + 0.03 v31_math_robustness + 0.08 v31_code_humaneval_plus + 0.05 v31_reasoning_logic_grid + 0.04 v31_reasoning_dyval_arith + 0.05 v31_long_context_ruler + 0.04 v31_knowledge_multi_hop_kg + 0.04 v31_ifeval_verifiable + 0.03 v31_truthfulness_calibration + 0.03 v31_consistency_paraphrase). The 0.50 freed comes from retiring or shrinking legacy axes the v31 axes replace: ifeval_bench 0.07→0.0 (replaced by v31_ifeval_verifiable), math_skill_group 0.10→0.0 + reasoning_skill_group 0.10→0.0 (replaced by v31 math + reasoning axes), code_skill_group 0.15→0.05, capability 0.10→0.05, length 0.08→0.05, degeneracy 0.10→0.05. Each v31 axis applies the IPT defence (arXiv 2604.15149) and the GSM-NoOp distractor methodology (arXiv 2410.05229) where applicable. Composite shadow version 31→32. v30.7 carry-overs: canary axes still computed for the king_canary_streak gate (margin 4pp, 1-round streak) and axis_correlation.json telemetry, but DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (CANARY_AXES_IN_COMPOSITE=0). See reports/2026-05-09-v31-axis-promotion.md and reports/2026-05-10-variance-reduction.md for full rationale.
